# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 160

This is turn 160. Turns 1 through 159 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 160.

# Turn 160 — multiplication-table certificate synthesis becomes executable

Turn 159 left one precise gap: the recurrence engine could validate supplied certificate DAGs but could not synthesize the framed root, active block, Fourier covers, or embedded exact-image children from an arbitrary multiplication table. 

I implemented that synthesis layer. The frozen 256-byte \(h_0\) submission remains unchanged, with SHA-256 `4f81b04ae00e737a244f2445d70b095333a427508958f11489e3cabdc0192fdc`. 

The compiler accepts a finite multiplication table and a marked normal \(2\)-subgroup \(L\), or computes \(O_2(G)\) automatically. It then:

- enumerates every generating tame frame of \(G/L\);
- detects the terminal case or constructs \(S\lhd P\), minimal \(K\), \(R=\Phi(K)\), \(A=K\cap S\), \(T_0=A/R\), and \(V=(K/R)/T_0\);
- computes the rank-one radical edge as an actual cocycle/coboundary problem;
- constructs every labeled scalar pushout and every zero-edge cover \(C^{\Delta_a}\);
- retains multiplicity by actual Fourier label and actual embedded subgroup;
- recursively evaluates the appropriate frozen recurrence and emits a serialized DAG.

The main uniform incidence simplifications are structural rather than casewise. For a central \(C_2\)-cover, every subgroup mapping onto the base is either the whole cover or a complement, so all exact-image subgroups are obtained by exhausting lifts of a fixed generating set. In the rank-one zero-edge branch,

\[
JK=Y,\qquad JR/R<Y/R
\]

forces

\[
(J\cap K)R/R\le T_0,
\]

and hence \(J\cap K\le A\). The compiler therefore enumerates subgroups of \(A\) together with lifts of generators of \(Y/K\), instead of constructing the full subgroup lattice of \(Y\).

Independent literal evaluation of the frozen \(h_0\) word matched the compiler’s raw surjection counts:

| Target | Compiler | Direct \(h_0\) evaluation | DAG nodes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| \(S_3\times C_2\) | 36 | 36 | 6 |
| \(A_4\) | 24 | 24 | 6 |
| \(\mathrm{SL}_2(3)\) | 0 | 0 | 14 |
| \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\) | 384 | 384 | 90 |

The \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\) run includes all six tame frames and recursively exercises terminal, \(R=1\), and rank-one zero-edge nodes.

For the order-\(320\) structural fixture \(K_{64}\!:\!C_5\), the compiler discovered

\[
|S|=|A|=|R|=4,\qquad |P|=|K|=64,
\]

classified the root as the \(|R|\ge4\) Fourier branch, and computed

\[
\left|(R^\vee)^Y\right|=4.
\]

The full recursive expansion of that fixture remains an exponential performance case, and the rank-one nonzero-edge branch is implemented but not yet exercised end-to-end by a clean current fixture. The private 5402-group verifier also remains unavailable; its required semantics are still the implicit tame relation, the \(2\)-core condition, and exponents reduced modulo \(M\). 

Artifacts:

[Turn 160 compiler bundle](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn160_compiler_bundle.zip)  
[Compiler source](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn160_compiler.py)  
[Machine-readable audit report](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn160_audit_report.json)  
[\(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\) 90-node certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/t160_GL2.json)  
[Byte-exact frozen \(h_0\) submission](sandbox:/mnt/data/q2_galois_h0_submission.txt)

Compiler SHA-256: `3ddfa0094a8e5dff4031248aa74595352edae449eb1319ac9d55e4c59bd7cc7b`.

**ESTABLISHED**: Built a uniform multiplication-table-to-recurrence-DAG compiler. It discovers the framed active block, computes radical edges, synthesizes labeled scalar and \(\Delta_a\) covers, enumerates actual embedded exact-image incidences, and reproduces independent literal \(h_0\) counts on four nontrivial targets, including 384 surjections onto \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\). Structurally identified the \(|R|\ge4\) branch for \(K_{64}\!:\!C_5\). The frozen word and checksum are unchanged.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. Full recursive expansion of larger \(|R|\ge4\) inputs remains exponential, the nonzero-edge branch lacks a completed end-to-end compiler fixture, and a literal schema adapter into Turn 159’s file-per-node validator remains to be added.

**NEXT**: Replace the remaining broad subgroup enumeration by a uniform extension-section enumerator parameterized by \(J\cap K\), then connect the emitted DAG directly to the Turn 159 lowest-failure validator and apply it to the first available verifier target.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
